sanskrit:
एवं ज्ञात्वा कृतं कर्म पूर्वैरपि मुमुक्षुभिः।
कुरु कर्मैव तस्मात्त्वं पूर्वैः पूर्वतरं कृतम्॥
transliteration:
evaṃ jñātvā kṛtaṃ karma pūrvair api mumukṣubhiḥ |
kuru karmaiva tasmāt tvaṃ pūrvaiḥ pūrvataraṃ kṛtam ||
literal_meaning: Thus having known, actions were performed by ancient seekers of liberation; therefore you too should perform action as done by the ancients before.
contextual_meaning: Krishna reassures Arjuna that even the most enlightened beings did not abandon action; they acted with full awareness, thus modeling that liberation is not escape from life but engagement without ego.
psychological_state: Cognitive dissonance reduction—Arjuna’s oscillation between withdrawal and duty is normalized; the psyche is invited to act while holding non-attachment.
trigger_scenario: A high-performing professional contemplates quitting to “find peace,” but realizes that true growth comes from doing the work with mindful presence, not from escape.
anchor_text: Act with awareness, as the ancients did, without ego.
